summary: Matched several previously stubbed functions in src/melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c for the Ty Fall/game-mode sequence, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes and exposing shared gmregtyfall scene/archive globals needed by the new implementations. The work builds GObj-based fog, light, camera, background, and character-display setup paths, adds character ordering/placement logic, and uses helper inlines/fake setters/tree-walks to preserve matching behavior.

key_files.reason: Main decompilation target; replaced stubs with implementations for gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, gm_801A9630 and refactored fn_801A7FB4 loops through inline helpers.
Updated public prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for newly matched functions.
Changed several scene/archive/static data objects from static to externally visible so gm_1A4C.c can reference shared assets.
Added extern declarations for gm_804D6798, gm_804D679C, gm_804D67A0, gm_804D67A4, gm_804D67A8, and gm_804D67AC, plus sc forward include for SceneDesc.

what_changed: Implemented gm_801A7B00, which creates and wires light, camera, main JObj, and character-display GObjs using HSD object callbacks, animation setup, and a deep JObj child/next walk for lb_8000C1C0/lb_8000C290 constraints.
Implemented gm_801A85E4, which places a JObj at randomized angular positions based on rank/index buckets, using cosf/sinf and fixed fallback positions for later indices.
Implemented gm_801A8D54, which builds a filtered list of valid character indices, special-cases Zelda/Sheik-related indices, and sorts entries using gm_80480AD0 random/order weights.

Implemented gm_801A9094, which creates display GObjs for sorted character/toy display entries, loads joints and material animations from gm_804D679C archive public addresses, attaches a stand joint, scales the root, applies animation frames, and calls gm_801A85E4 for placement.
Implemented gm_801A9630, which initializes per-character random weights, then creates fog, light, camera, background, character display, and constrained character JObj GObjs for the sequence.
Split repeated live-character-counting logic in fn_801A7FB4 into two inline helper functions, apparently to satisfy matching/codegen constraints while keeping the same loop behavior.
Added required includes for ft/forward.h, gm_1BA8.h, tydisplay.h, archive/cobj/gobj/gobjproc/random, gmregtyfall.static.h, lb_00B0.h, lb_00F9.h, sc/types.h, and MSL math support.
Converted gm_1A4C.h prototypes for gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, and gm_801A9630 from placeholder unknown signatures to typed void signatures.
Made gmregtyfall scene/archive globals externally linkable and declared them in gmregtyfall.h for reuse by gm_1A4C.c.
smart_moves: Kept asset pointers such as gm_804D6798, gm_804D679C, and gm_804D67A0-gm_804D67AC owned in gmregtyfall.c but exposed through extern declarations instead of duplicating or inventing new storage.
Used small inline wrappers/helpers, including gm_801A8114_inline and duplicated fn_801A7FB4_inline variants, to influence inlining/code generation without changing the apparent runtime logic.
Preserved decomp-sensitive ternary boolean patterns like `un_803048C0(gm_801A659C(i)) ? true : false`, which are often necessary for matching compiler output even when redundant in normal C.
Used explicit JObj tree walks rather than abstracting traversal, which likely mirrors the original fixed asset hierarchy and helps retain instruction ordering.
Added concrete typed prototypes once function shapes were known, improving downstream type propagation and reducing placeholder usage.
decomp_lessons: For game-mode setup functions, matching often requires reconstructing the exact sequence of GObj creation, object attachment, GX link setup, animation requests, animation evaluation, and proc registration.
When two translation units share assets initialized in one file, remove `static` only for the specific required globals and add precise extern declarations in the corresponding header.
Do not simplify apparently redundant boolean ternaries, loop initializers such as `for (i = count; i < 0x1A; i++)`, or empty null branches if they are present for codegen matching.
Fixed-depth asset hierarchy traversal can be part of the match; avoid replacing repeated HSD_JObjGetChild/HSD_JObjGetNext calls with generic helpers unless the generated code still matches.
Small inline functions or wrapper calls can be useful matching tools when repeated source logic would otherwise generate nonmatching code in different call-site contexts.
Typed headers should be updated alongside decomped functions so later files can rely on real signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions and data, e.g. gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_80480A00, gm_80480AD0, gm_804D67B8.
Local helper names used the existing function-address plus `_inline` suffix pattern, e.g. fn_801A7FB4_inline, fn_801A7FB4_inline2, and gm_801A8114_inline.
Temporary fake setter names were preserved for matching-specific behavior in Ty Fall code, e.g. fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z and fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z.
Public declarations in headers kept the file's address-comment convention, such as `/* 1A7B00 */ void gm_801A7B00(void);` and `/* 4D6798 */ extern HSD_Joint* gm_804D6798;`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations with exact sizes such as 0x30, 0x20, 0x68, 0x88, and 8 to shape stack frames.
Introduced inline helpers for repeated counting loops in fn_801A7FB4 to control emitted code at two separate sites.
Kept manual casts to HSD_CameraDescPerspective*, HSD_JObj*, u8 object-kind constants, and const char* archive names where the diff shows they were needed.
Included MSL math locally before gm_801A85E4-era code to use cosf/sinf in the expected form.
Used explicit `__assert("jobj.h", line, "joint"/"jobj")` calls around HSD_JObjLoadJoint setup to mirror baselib macro behavior.
Maintained unusual compare expression in sort logic, `(gm_80480AD0[sp84[i]] < gm_80480AD0[sp84[j]] ? 1 : 2) == 2`, instead of simplifying it.

review_feedback.feedback: No review comments or review bodies were present in the provided slice; there is no explicit reviewer rationale to extract.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename gm_801A7B00, gm_801A85E4, gm_801A8D54, gm_801A9094, or gm_801A9630 to semantic Ty Fall names.
Search for subsequent cleanup of fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet* helpers or replacement with regular HSD_JObjSet* calls.
Look for PRs touching gmregtyfall.static.h or gm_804D6798/gm_804D679C to clarify ownership of Ty Fall scene/archive globals.
Check whether gm_80480A00 and gm_80480AD0 later receive struct names or become part of a typed Ty Fall display state.
